description_long: This object is a vintage-style CRT television with a boxy, rectangular form and a slightly curved glass screen. The front panel features a large, dark display area surrounded by a thick, light-colored bezel, with a set of rotary dials and speaker grille on the right side. The casing is primarily composed of a faux woodgrain finish on the top and sides, while the back is made of textured dark plastic with ventilation slots and a product information label. The television sits on a small black base, and the rear panel includes visible input/output ports. The overall design is reminiscent of consumer electronics from the late 20th century, with tactile controls and robust construction. This type of television would commonly be found in urban homes, garages, or even discarded on sidewalks.
description: A vintage CRT television with a woodgrain exterior, curved glass screen, and rotary dials on the front panel. The back features a dark plastic casing with vents and a product label.
description_view_0: The front view shows the curved glass screen, thick bezel, rotary dials, and speaker grille, highlighting the main interface and controls.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the woodgrain finish and the depth of the television's casing, with a protruding back.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the dark plastic back panel, ventilation slots, product label, and input/output ports at the bottom.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the woodgrain finish and the overall boxy profile of the television.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, light gray plastic bezel with a matte finish and integrated rotary dials on the right side.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and buttons on the front right edge
